item.title: Go, build for a different architecture
item.description: It happened that I needed to (re)build a small program for an i386 machine. This was about the same time I realized we still had those… It’s very easy but since I’ll not be using this a lot I wanted to document it. First of all, build the correct Go compiler. I’m on (Ubuntu) amd64 and these instructions explain how to get the i386 build. (you can replace all (i)386 with amd64 to do the reverse.

item.title: First post with Hugo
item.description: I was a little bored with the previous look and decided to play around with some other static HTML generators. I did like that I could edit the website directly in GitHub but I still decided to move away from the current setup with “Jekyll Now”. It’s more fun to edit files in atom anyway. I’ve been playing with Go lately and decided to look for a static HTML generator in Go.
